design_philosophy.core_loop: Select from four thematic pinball tables, complete table-specific objectives (lighting letters, overtaking cars, winning game show prizes, completing modes), accumulate high scores across multiple balls, and compete for position on high-score lists
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Precision ball control versus randomness of physics; risk/reward of attempting high-value shots; managing limited balls across multiple tables with distinct mechanics
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Mastering arcade pinball tables with distinct personalities and gameplay rules; achieving high scores and beating competitors on leaderboards; progressing through increasingly difficult modes on each table
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Each of the four tables has radically different objectives and mechanics (amusement park letter collection vs. car racing vs. game show vs. haunted house modes), forcing players to learn distinct strategies rather than apply universal pinball skill. Tables feature thematic special ramps and unique progression systems. The game treats high-score tables as the primary competitive metric, not narrative progression.

difficulty.lives_system: Variable number of balls selectable by player before match start
difficulty.checkpointing: Persistent high-score tracking via cartridge EEPROM; automatic saving of player settings and high-scores
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players can adjust difficulty levels and customize the number of balls per game, allowing self-tuned challenge scaling. Each table presents distinct difficulty curves through progressive mode unlocks

progression.upgrade_system: Progressive mode unlocks within each table (e.g., Stones 'N Bones requires lighting eight successively more rewarding modes by completing STONE-BONE targets, then cycling continuously). High-scoring rounds triggered by achieving specific objectives (Miles, Skills numbers). Extra ball awards at specific score thresholds
progression.unlock_mechanism: Objectives completed on-table unlock special ramps and high-scoring rounds. Extra balls awarded when exceeding highest score on list or succeeding at match end
replayability_hooks: Four distinct tables with unique mechanics encourage specialized mastery
High-score leaderboard persistence via EEPROM drives competitive replays
Each table features special ramps that accumulate bonuses (Cyclones, Miles, Skills, Screams) creating distinct progression paths
Customizable difficulty and ball count allows players to self-scale challenge for improvement attempts

world_building_techniques: Table theming (Party Land amusement park, Speed Devils car racing, Billion Dollar Gameshow, Stones 'N Bones haunted house) establishes distinct worlds
Objectives reflect table themes (PARTY/CRAZY letters, overtaking cars, winning game show prizes, completing haunted house modes)
Visual and mechanical design reinforce table identity rather than explicit narrative

environmental_storytelling: Party Land: amusement park setting with PARTY/CRAZY objective framing
Speed Devils: competitive car racing context with overtaking mechanics
Billion Dollar Gameshow: prize-winning game show atmosphere
Stones 'N Bones: haunted house aesthetic with escalating mode difficulty
